prerequisites: A running IBM Db2 instance reachable from the connector over the network, with its host and port (the port the Db2 instance listens on).
The target database name (the `db` config field) to read from.
A Db2 user account (`username`) and its `password` that can connect to the database and read the tables you want to sync.

TLS/SSL settings for the `encryption` config field if you connect over an encrypted channel: the server's SSL certificate (`encryption.ssl_certificate`) and, where a key store is used, its password (`encryption.key_store_password`).
Any extra JDBC connection properties you need, supplied through the optional `jdbc_url_params` field.

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Connect to the Db2 instance with a user ID and password. Set `username` and the secret `password` config fields. Per the IBM Db2 authentication guide, Db2 verifies the supplied user ID and password through its security facility before allowing the connection. The user must be authorized to read the target database and tables.

authMethods.name: TLS/SSL connection encryption (optional)
authMethods.summary: Encrypt the connection to Db2 using the `encryption` config field. Provide the server's SSL certificate via the secret `encryption.ssl_certificate` field and, when a key store is used, the secret `encryption.key_store_password`. This secures the transport for the username and password authentication above; it is not a separate identity mechanism.

setupSteps.title: Locate the Db2 instance connection details
setupSteps.body: Identify the `host` and `port` of the Db2 instance and the name of the target database (`db`). These must be reachable from wherever the connector runs.

setupSteps.title: Prepare a Db2 user with read access
setupSteps.body: Use or create a Db2 user account and note its `username` and `password`. The account must be able to connect to the `db` database and read the tables you intend to sync. Db2 authenticates this user ID and password through its security facility as described in the IBM Db2 authentication guide.

setupSteps.title: Configure connection encryption (if required)
setupSteps.body: If you connect to Db2 over TLS/SSL, populate the `encryption` field with the server's SSL certificate (`encryption.ssl_certificate`) and, where a key store is involved, the key store password (`encryption.key_store_password`).

setupSteps.title: Add optional JDBC parameters
setupSteps.body: If your environment needs extra JDBC connection properties, set them in the optional `jdbc_url_params` field. Leave it empty otherwise.

setupSteps.title: Enter the configuration in the connector
setupSteps.body: Fill in `host`, `port`, `db`, `username`, `password`, and `encryption`, then save. The connector uses these to open a JDBC connection to the Db2 database and authenticate.
